description: Lightweight self-aware AI agent for Arch Linux. Core single-threaded tool-use loop with optional Telegram long-poll adapter, native news briefing pipeline (fetch/filter/summarize/fallback, cached daily digests, optional Telegram delivery), native web retrieval tools (`web_search`/`web_read`) with SearxNG/Brave/DDG provider support, an external coding-worker delegation/control layer (`delegate_code_task`, `worker_start/send/poll/cancel/status/list/approve/reconcile`) backed by persistent Archon worker sessions, and a lightweight call-control plane (`voice_service_*`, `call_mission_*`) backed by file-based mission state plus an optional same-repo FastAPI voice runtime (`services/archon_voice`) supporting both scripted Twilio calls and a realtime Twilio Media Streams <-> Deepgram Voice Agent bridge path. Archon core remains stdlib-first and framework-free; the voice runtime is isolated outside `archon/`. 4 core dependencies plus service-local optional deps.
install: pip install -e . (editable mode, instant source changes)

architecture.pattern: Single-threaded core tool-use loop (Claude Code pattern) with optional background transport adapter threads
architecture.flow: cli.py -> agent.py -> llm.py -> API. Optional telegram adapter (adapters/telegram.py) long-polls Telegram, maintains per-chat Agent sessions, reuses the same agent/tool stack, and also exposes deterministic `/news` + `/news_status` commands backed by archon/news/*. `archon news` routes through archon/news/* for stateful daily briefing orchestration (cache lookup -> fetchers -> pipeline -> LLM summarize with fallback -> optional Telegram send -> state save). Agent web retrieval uses native tools backed by archon/web/* (`web_search` provider abstraction + `web_read` fetch/extract). External coding delegation/control routes through worker tools -> `archon/workers/*` (router + Codex/Claude/OpenCode CLI adapters + streaming subprocess helper + persistent session/event store + background runtime) with normalized task/result models, Archon session IDs, turn-based follow-up via vendor session resume IDs (Claude/OpenCode), runtime event polling for background runs, and manual stale-session repair via `worker_reconcile`.
architecture.llm_format: Internal message format follows Anthropic's schema (tool_use/tool_result blocks). tool_result also carries tool_name for Google provider compatibility. OpenAI and Google messages are converted on the fly in llm.py.
architecture.providers: Three providers: 'anthropic' (native SDK), 'openai' (native SDK, supports base_url for OpenRouter etc), 'google' (native google-genai SDK, avoids thought_signature issues with OpenAI-compat endpoint).
architecture.prompt_system: Prompts loaded from prompts/ directory at runtime (Agent Zero insight). Variables injected by prompt.py.

modules.archon/cli.py: Click CLI entry/composition layer. Commands exposed: chat (interactive REPL), run (single-shot), telegram (long-poll bot), news (run/preview/status), history (list/show/delete), system (profile), self (info/recover), memory (search/list/read). `archon/cli.py` now mainly wires modules together and keeps wrapper functions/callback seams stable for tests. Logic is split across `archon/cli_ui.py`, `archon/cli_commands.py`, `archon/cli_input.py`, `archon/cli_repl_commands.py`, `archon/cli_runtime.py`, `archon/cli_news_commands.py`, `archon/cli_memory_commands.py`, `archon/cli_history_commands.py`, `archon/cli_interactive_commands.py`, and `archon/cli_self_commands.py`.
modules.archon/cli_ui.py: Terminal UI helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: ANSI style constants, readline-safe prompt builder (`_make_readline_prompt`), spinner class (`_Spinner`), and response/stat summary formatters (`_format_chat_response`, `_format_turn_stats`, `_format_session_summary`). Keeps CLI behavior unchanged while reducing `cli.py` hotspot size.
modules.archon/cli_commands.py: Slash-command helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: model catalog and slash command metadata plus reusable helpers for slash completion (`slash_completer`), interactive pickers (`run_picker`, `pick_slash_command`), and subvalue construction (`build_model_set_subvalues`, `build_slash_subvalues`). Includes top-level slash metadata for `/calls`, `/profile`, `/mcp`, `/jobs`, and `/job`, so readline/picker discovery stays aligned with REPL command handling. `archon/cli.py` keeps thin wrapper functions to preserve existing test monkeypatch seams.
modules.archon/cli_input.py: Paste input helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: paste command detection, multiline paste collection with end-markers, bracketed-paste start detection, and bracketed-paste payload collection. `archon/cli.py` keeps wrapper functions/constants for compatibility while delegating behavior here.
modules.archon/cli_repl_commands.py: REPL command-handler module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `/model`, `/model-list`, `/model-set`, `/calls`, `/profile`, `/mcp`, `/jobs`, and `/job` slash command handlers plus top-level slash dispatch helpers, helper functions for provider credential resolution, job-summary lookup, and `[calls].enabled` TOML patching. `/mcp` exposes terminal inspection for configured MCP servers and advertised MCP tools via the shared MCP client. Implemented with injected dependencies or stable module-level seams so `archon/cli.py` wrappers preserve test monkeypatch behavior and runtime wiring.
modules.archon/cli_runtime.py: Runtime/wiring helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: transient model-runtime error classification (`is_model_runtime_error`), fully wired agent construction (`make_agent`), Telegram adapter factory (`make_telegram_adapter`), and compact news result rendering (`print_news_result`). `make_agent()` now passes the loaded `Config` into `ToolRegistry` so config-backed built-ins like MCP tools share the same runtime settings as the agent. Functions remain dependency-injected so `archon/cli.py` can keep compatibility wrappers and behavior stable.
modules.archon/cli_news_commands.py: News command-body helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `news run`, `news preview`, and `news status` logic with injected dependencies (`load_config`, `run_news`, state-path/json rendering, output/exit hooks) so CLI wrappers stay thin and behavior-preserving.
modules.archon/cli_memory_commands.py: Memory command-body helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `memory search|list|read` logic with injected dependencies and ANSI formatting inputs, preserving CLI output behavior while reducing `cli.py` complexity.
modules.archon/cli_history_commands.py: History command-body helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `history list|show|delete` logic with injected dependencies for session IO, timestamp formatting, and output styling, keeping `cli.py` wrappers thin and test seams stable.
modules.archon/cli_interactive_commands.py: Interactive command-body helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `chat`, `run`, `telegram`, and `system` command logic with dependency-injected IO/runtime hooks (readline, input, echo, adapter/agent factories, timing, exit behavior) so behavior remains unchanged while shrinking `cli.py` complexity. The chat REPL now treats `/mcp` as a handled local action alongside `/calls`, `/profile`, `/jobs`, and `/job`, preventing MCP inspection commands from falling through into the model loop.
modules.archon/cli_self_commands.py: Self command-body helper module extracted from `archon/cli.py`: `self info` and `self recover` logic with injected source-discovery, prompt/output, subprocess, and exit hooks to keep wrapper/test seams stable.
modules.archon/agent.py: Core loop. Agent.run(msg) now trims overly long conversation history at turn start using a lightweight dual budget (message-count sliding window plus approximate char-budget trimming as a token-free proxy; defaults are conservative and configurable via `agent.history_*` settings) before appending the new user message, then best-effort auto-queues explicit user preference statements into the memory inbox (e.g. 'I prefer ...', 'use ... by default'), builds a per-turn augmented system prompt with best-effort indexed memory prefetch snippets (`[Retrieved Memory]` block from `memory.prefetch_for_query()` including path/kind/scope plus stability/confidence/last_modified metadata), then calls llm.chat() in a loop, executes tool calls via tools.py, appends results (including tool_name), and repeats until text response or iteration limit. `Agent.run()` includes a small best-effort retry wrapper for transient LLM/provider errors (e.g. 503/429/timeout-like failures), and `Agent.run_stream()` now applies the same lightweight transient retry policy while collecting stream chunks internally (so retries do not duplicate user-visible output) before using the final response. Tool-call/result stderr traces now include an optional agent log label plus per-turn IDs (`turn=tNNN`) and a higher-contrast color split (bright cyan calls, readable light result lines, dim truncation markers) for easier debugging when terminal and Telegram activity interleave in one process. Exposes `on_thinking` and `on_tool_call` callback hooks for transport-level UX (typing indicators, spinners). It now also repairs malformed tool-call history fragments before each turn (drops dangling assistant `tool_use` turns/orphan user `tool_result` turns) and rolls back appended assistant tool-use turns if tool execution hooks raise, keeping provider function-call sequences valid across retries/next turns.
modules.archon/adapters/telegram.py: Telegram Bot API long-poll adapter using stdlib urllib only (via shared `archon/adapters/telegram_client.py` helpers). Supports user allowlist, one Agent session per chat, background thread start/stop, `/help`/`/reset`, deterministic `/news` + `/news_status` commands that call the native news backend directly (bypassing the LLM loop), and per-chat dangerous-action approvals with request-scoped pending approvals + Telegram inline buttons (`Approve Request`, `Allow 15m`, `Deny`) plus `/approve`/`/deny` fallbacks. Dangerous-action blocks create a pending approval request, send a single approval prompt, and suppress the duplicate generic `Command rejected by safety gate` reply; approved requests auto-replay the original Telegram message, and a 15-minute elevated chat mode can be granted from the approval prompt while FORBIDDEN actions remain blocked. Legacy `/approve_next` and `/approvals on|off` controls still work. On startup it performs a one-shot `getUpdates(timeout=0)` sync and advances the polling offset to skip queued stale Telegram messages (preventing replay of old commands after Archon restarts while offset persistence remains in-memory only). Telegram exchanges are persisted via the existing `archon/history.py` JSONL backend using chat-scoped session IDs (`tg-<chat_id>-<timestamp>`). Sends `sendChatAction(typing)` on every LLM call and tool call via agent callback hooks for responsive UX. Telegram voice/audio support now includes Phase A+B+C behavior: inbound `voice`/`audio` messages are downloaded, transcribed with Gemini (`archon/audio/stt.py`), routed through the normal text agent pipeline, and then receive a best-effort spoken reply using Gemini TTS—preferably as a Telegram voice-note (`sendVoice`, OGG/Opus via ffmpeg conversion) with automatic fallback to a WAV document attachment if conversion/upload fails. When a new dangerous action is blocked while one pending approval already exists, the pending request is updated in-place to the latest blocked message/preview and the approval prompt is refreshed, so `/approve` and inline approval replay the current request instead of an earlier stale one.
modules.archon/adapters/telegram_client.py: Shared stdlib Telegram Bot API client helpers used by both the long-poll Telegram adapter and the news runner: POST/JSON/error handling (`TelegramBotClient.api_call`), `sendMessage` chunking/send helpers (optional `reply_markup` plus single-message `send_message()`), `sendChatAction(typing)` wrapper, callback/edit helpers (`answerCallbackQuery`, `editMessageText`) for Telegram inline approval UX, Telegram media file helpers (`getFile`, `download_file`) for voice/audio input, and stdlib multipart uploads for both `sendDocument` bytes and `sendVoice` bytes used by Telegram TTS reply paths.
modules.archon/audio/__init__.py: Audio helper package exporting Gemini STT (`transcribe_audio_bytes`) and Gemini TTS (`synthesize_speech_wav`) helpers used by Telegram voice features.
modules.archon/audio/stt.py: Gemini-based audio transcription helper for Telegram voice/audio messages: resolves Google API key from explicit arg/config/env, sends inline audio bytes via `google-genai` (`Part.from_bytes`) with a plain-transcript prompt, and extracts transcript text robustly from `response.text` or candidate parts.
modules.archon/adapters/telegram_approvals.py: Pure helper functions/constants for Telegram request-scoped approval UX: callback-data parsing/building, inline button markup generation, approval prompt/status text formatting, command-preview truncation, safety-gate rejection heuristics, and a best-effort `answerCallbackQuery` wrapper used to keep `telegram.py` callback handling less repetitive.
modules.archon/llm.py: LLMClient wraps anthropic, openai, and google-genai SDKs. Three providers: 'anthropic', 'openai', 'google'. Normalizes responses into LLMResponse dataclass (text, tool_calls, raw_content, token counts). Supports both non-streaming (`chat()`) and streaming (`chat_stream()`) modes; streaming yields text deltas then a final LLMResponse. Anthropic and OpenAI have native streaming; Google falls back to non-streaming (thought_signature complexity). Google provider uses native genai.Client and handles Content/Part conversion; now prevalidates missing Google API key and raises a clear ValueError. OpenAI provider supports base_url and now correctly preserves multiple tool_result messages when converting Anthropic-style history to OpenAI chat format (fixes prior multi-tool-result drop bug). Message conversion methods: _convert_message_to_openai, _convert_message_to_google, _convert_tools_to_openai, _convert_tools_to_google.
modules.archon/tools.py: Public `ToolRegistry` module and stable import surface (`from archon.tools import ToolRegistry`). Contains registry plumbing (`register`, `execute`, schema export), worker session-affinity helpers (repo+worker -> session_id), and `_register_builtins()` that delegates to modular tool registrars including content, MCP (`mcp_call`), call-service lifecycle (`voice_service_*`), call-mission (`call_mission_*`), and worker tools. The registry now carries the loaded `Config` so config-backed built-ins can avoid reloading global config during one agent session.
modules.archon/tooling/__init__.py: Re-exports registration helpers used by `ToolRegistry._register_builtins()` (filesystem, memory, content, MCP, call-service, call-mission, worker tool registrars).
modules.archon/tooling/common.py: Small shared helpers for local tool modules: text truncation and best-effort git stash auto-save before self-modification.
modules.archon/tooling/filesystem_tools.py: Registers shell/read_file/write_file/edit_file/list_dir with identical behavior to the prior inlined closures (safety gating, self-modification checks, output formatting).
modules.archon/tooling/memory_tools.py: Registers memory_read/memory_write/memory_lookup and memory inbox review tools using the file-based memory backend. `memory_read` list mode now checks `memory_store.MEMORY_DIR` at runtime (instead of an imported static config constant) so monkeypatched/test-scoped memory dirs behave correctly. `memory_inbox_decide` supports lightweight apply modes: default `append` and `replace_section` (with `section_heading`) for safer updates to canonical markdown memory files.
modules.archon/tooling/content_tools.py: Registers `news_brief`, `web_search`, and `web_read` with the same config loading, caching/delivery behavior, and text formatting as before the extraction.
modules.archon/tooling/mcp_tools.py: Registers the built-in `mcp_call` tool. It instantiates the shared `archon.mcp.client.MCPClient` against the registry's loaded config, calls a configured read-only MCP server/tool, and formats the result into Archon-friendly string output (`mcp_server`, `mcp_tool`, `mcp_mode`, optional truncation/error markers, then content). Server-specific allow/deny is enforced in the Agent loop via `evaluate_mcp_policy` before execution.
modules.archon/mcp/client.py: Read-only MCP client for Archon's first runtime slice. Validates configured servers, exposes `list_tools()` and `call_tool()` helpers, supports injected transports for tests, and provides a default `stdio` JSON-RPC transport that spawns the configured server command per invocation, sends `initialize`, then `tools/list` or `tools/call`, caps returned content, and returns string-safe errors on lifecycle/protocol failures. The stdio transport now merges per-server environment variables from config (`MCPServerConfig.env`) into the child process environment so hosted/read-only MCP servers such as Exa can receive API keys without shell wrappers. This remains intentionally lightweight: no persistent sessions, no prompts/resources, and no write-capable server support.
modules.archon/tooling/worker_tools.py: Compatibility facade + monkeypatch surface for worker/delegation tools. Imports worker/session-store/runtime functions (so existing tests/patches still target `archon.tooling.worker_tools.*`), retains delegate/session helper routines (execution-mode planning, session-reuse detection, runtime quiet-time calculation), and delegates actual tool registration to `archon/tooling/worker_session_tools.py` and `archon/tooling/worker_delegate_tools.py`.
modules.archon/tooling/worker_session_tools.py: Thin facade for worker session tool registration. Composes `worker_session_query_tools.py` (status/list/poll) and `worker_session_action_tools.py` (start/send/cancel/approve/reconcile) while preserving the same `register_worker_session_tools(...)->{'worker_send': ...}` contract used by delegate-tool registration and existing monkeypatch-driven tests.
modules.archon/tooling/worker_session_query_tool: Registers worker session query/status tools (`worker_status`, `worker_list`, `worker_poll`) extracted from the previous monolithic `worker_session_tools.py`. Uses late-bound lookups through the `worker_tools` namespace to preserve monkeypatch-based test behavior.
modules.archon/tooling/worker_session_action_too: Registers worker session action/control tools (`worker_start`, `worker_send`, `worker_cancel`, `worker_approve`, `worker_reconcile`) extracted from the previous monolithic `worker_session_tools.py`. Preserves late-bound `worker_tools` namespace lookups and returns `{'worker_send': worker_send}` for delegate-tool integration.
modules.archon/tooling/worker_delegate_tools.py: Registers `delegate_code_task` and the shared `_run_and_record_delegated_task` helper extracted from `worker_tools.py`, including sticky same-repo session reuse/reroute logic. Uses late-bound lookups through the `worker_tools` module namespace to preserve existing monkeypatch behavior in tests.
modules.archon/safety.py: Three-tier command classification: SAFE (read-only), DANGEROUS (state-changing, needs TTY confirm), FORBIDDEN (catastrophic, always blocked). Uses shlex.split, handles pipes/chains by classifying each segment. Includes a special-case override so `sed` remains read-mostly SAFE but in-place edit flags (`-i`, `-Ei`, `--in-place`) classify as DANGEROUS. User overrides via ~/.config/archon/safety.toml (allow/deny lists). Self-modification of safety.py is FORBIDDEN.
modules.archon/config.py: Loads ~/.config/archon/config.toml via tomllib. Dataclasses include LLM/Agent/Safety/Telegram/Web plus nested NewsConfig and call-control config (`calls`: `CallsConfig` with `voice_service` + `twilio`). `AgentConfig` includes lightweight context-budget knobs (`history_max_messages`, `history_trim_to_messages`, `history_max_chars`, `history_trim_to_chars`). MCP server config now supports per-server `env` maps with `${VAR}` interpolation from process environment so read-only stdio MCP servers can receive API keys without storing secrets directly in Archon config. Env vars override core provider/Telegram/Web settings. XDG paths (including news state/cache and call mission/event state dirs) are defined here.
modules.archon/news/models.py: Dataclasses for normalized news items, rendered digests, and runner results with JSON-friendly `to_dict()` helpers.
modules.archon/news/state.py: XDG-backed news state/idempotency helpers (`state.json`) plus cached digest artifact helpers (`~/.cache/archon/news/digest-YYYY-MM-DD.json`), including daily run gating (`should_run_today`).
modules.archon/news/runner.py: News runner orchestration: enforces daily state gate, reuses/builds cached daily digests (`get_or_build_news_digest`), fetches enabled sources, applies filtering/ranking pipeline, summarizes via LLM with deterministic fallback, builds final digest, optionally sends directly via Telegram Bot API using the shared `archon/adapters/telegram_client.py` helper, and persists run state.
modules.archon/news/fetchers.py: Stdlib HTTP fetchers for Hacker News, GitHub Search API, Hugging Face papers page (regex scrape), and r/LocalLLaMA. Includes keyword/blocklist filtering defaults (ported from OpenClaw) and per-source error isolation.
modules.archon/news/pipeline.py: Filtering/dedup/ranking helpers for news items: source thresholds, URL dedupe, prefilter cap, and final digest item selection.
modules.archon/news/summarize.py: Archon-native news summarization prompt builder and LLM summarizer with retries/backoff, plus deterministic fallback digest builder for provider failures.
modules.archon/news/formatting.py: Digest formatting helpers: standard header/footer wrapper, Telegram-safe truncation, and chunk splitting.
modules.archon/web/search.py: Lightweight web search backend with provider abstraction: SearxNG JSON, Brave Search API, and DuckDuckGo HTML scraping fallback. Normalizes results and applies optional domain filters.
modules.archon/web/read.py: HTTP(S) page fetch and readable-text extraction using stdlib urllib + HTMLParser. Blocks localhost URLs to keep `web_read` scoped to public web sources.
modules.archon/workers/__init__.py: Worker delegation export surface (`WorkerTask`, `WorkerResult`, session-store helpers including live event append + manual reconcile, background runtime helpers, `run_worker_task`, formatters).
modules.archon/workers/base.py: Typed models for delegated coding runs (`WorkerTask`, `WorkerEvent`, `WorkerResult`) with JSON serialization helpers, resume-vendor-session + Archon-session support on `WorkerTask`, a `WorkerExecObserver` callback protocol for runtime-managed subprocess lifecycle/output streaming, and normalized text formatting for tool responses. Uses shared worker formatting helpers from `archon/workers/common.py`.
modules.archon/workers/router.py: Worker router that normalizes worker names (`auto`, `codex`, `claude_code`, `opencode`), chooses an available backend for `auto`, preserves Archon session IDs when normalizing tasks, and dispatches to adapter implementations (Codex, Claude Code, and OpenCode one-shot adapters implemented).
modules.archon/workers/common.py: Shared worker-adapter utility helpers (low-complexity dedupe): inline/report truncation, first-nonempty-line extraction, and standardized CLI run summary formatting used by Codex/Claude/OpenCode adapters and worker result formatting.
modules.archon/workers/codex_cli.py: Codex CLI adapter. Runs `codex exec --json` non-interactively with mode-based sandboxing, captures `--output-last-message`, parses JSONL events, and returns normalized `WorkerResult` output. Supports an optional runtime observer path that switches to streaming `Popen` execution for PID tracking/output events/cancellation. Uses shared helper utilities from `archon/workers/common.py` for summary/error formatting.
modules.archon/workers/claude_code_cli.py: Claude Code CLI adapter (Phase 2). Runs `claude -p --output-format stream-json`, parses stream JSON events, extracts a vendor session ID, and supports follow-up turns via `--resume <vendor_session_id>`. For implement/debug, it can enable a permission broker (`--permission-prompt-tool` + temp `--mcp-config`) when the installed Claude CLI supports the flag and an Archon session ID is available; otherwise it returns a clear unsupported message. Supports an optional runtime observer path (streaming `Popen`) for background runtime PID/output/cancel integration. Uses shared helper utilities from `archon/workers/common.py` for summary/error formatting.
modules.archon/workers/opencode_cli.py: OpenCode CLI adapter (Phase 1.6). Runs `opencode run --format json`, parses JSON event output, extracts a best-effort vendor session ID/final message (including real OpenCode `sessionID` fields and `text` events with nested `part.text`), and returns normalized `WorkerResult` output. Supports turn-based continuation by forwarding `WorkerTask.resume_vendor_session_id` as `--session <id>` and supports an optional runtime observer path (streaming `Popen`) for background runtime PID/output/cancel integration. Uses shared helper utilities from `archon/workers/common.py` for summary/error formatting.
modules.archon/workers/claude_permission_mcp.py: Minimal stdio MCP server used by Claude Code `--permission-prompt-tool`. Exposes one `permission_prompt` tool that records approval requests into the Archon worker session store, waits for `worker_approve` decisions, and returns JSON allow/deny payloads back to Claude.
modules.archon/workers/session_store.py: Persistent XDG-backed worker session/event store facade for delegated runs. Writes session metadata/results to `~/.local/state/archon/workers/sessions/*.json` and normalized events to `.../events/*.jsonl`, supports provisional session reservation before launch, live event appends while a worker is running, multi-turn session append + Archon-side cancellation markers, preserves cancelled status across late background finalization, stores approval requests/decisions (`pending/approved/denied`) for the worker approval broker, and uses a process-local lock + atomic payload writes to reduce concurrent write corruption/races. Follow-up turn appends update persisted session mode to avoid mode drift after review/analyze -> implement/debug continuations. `session_store.py` now delegates data models, formatting, and worker-summary memory-capture logic to extracted support modules while preserving the public API and monkeypatch seams (`_maybe_queue_worker_summary_candidate`, `_resolve_worker_summary_target`). Includes `reconcile_worker_session()` for repairing orphaned stuck records.
modules.archon/workers/session_store_models.py: Extracted data models for the worker session store (`WorkerSessionRecord`, `WorkerApprovalRequest`) with JSON-friendly `to_dict()` / `from_dict()` helpers. Re-exported via `session_store.py` to preserve import compatibility.
modules.archon/workers/session_store_format.py: Formatting helpers extracted from `session_store.py` for worker session records, approval lists, and worker session list output (`format_worker_session_record`, `format_worker_approvals`, `format_worker_session_list`).
modules.archon/workers/session_store_memory_capt: Best-effort worker-summary -> memory-inbox capture helpers extracted from `session_store.py`: filters low-value summaries, resolves canonical project memory targets conservatively via indexed lookup, and queues `worker_summary` inbox candidates without coupling the session store directly to the memory backend at import time.
modules.archon/workers/runtime.py: Phase 2 in-process background worker runtime. Launches delegated worker runs in daemon threads, tracks active run state plus live process metadata (PID/process_state/returncode/last output/cancel signal), records runtime process/output events into the worker session event log during execution, and records final results back into the worker session store. Enables non-blocking worker_start/worker_send with same-channel approval handling via polling + worker_approve and best-effort terminate/kill on worker_cancel.
modules.archon/workers/subprocess_exec.py: Streaming/cancellable subprocess helper used by worker adapters when a runtime observer is present. Runs `Popen` with stdout/stderr reader threads, emits line callbacks, supports cooperative cancellation/timeouts with terminate->kill escalation, and returns captured stdout/stderr/returncode plus timeout/cancel flags.
modules.archon/system.py: Gathers system profile (OS, kernel, CPU, RAM, GPU, shell, packages, docker). Caches to ~/.cache/archon/system.json with 1hr TTL. format_profile() produces ~6 line summary for prompt.
modules.archon/memory.py: CRUD + indexing for markdown memory files in ~/.local/share/archon/memory/. Supports read(), write(), search() (grep-based), summary() (first 50 lines of MEMORY.md for prompt), list_files(), a lightweight machine-readable memory index (`memory_index.json`) with rebuild/load/lookup helpers, a `prefetch_for_query()` helper that returns compact ranked memory snippets for prompt-time retrieval (including stability/confidence/last_modified metadata), and a Phase 1 review queue (`memory_inbox.jsonl`) with add/list/decide(apply|reject) helpers. Includes explicit user-preference auto-capture helpers that queue obvious preference statements into the inbox. Inbox decisions now guard terminal states so duplicate applies are idempotent and invalid apply/reject transitions are rejected instead of duplicating memory writes, and support a minimal section-aware apply mode (`replace_section`) that safely replaces a markdown section by heading line instead of always appending. `write()` normalizes known paths (for example `system-profile.md` -> `profiles/system.md`), maintains a small auto-managed pointer block in `MEMORY.md` (project registry + system profile), preserves backward compatibility for legacy reads (`memory_read("system-profile.md")` before migration), and best-effort refreshes the index so newly saved memory is discoverable via indexed lookup.
modules.archon/introspect.py: Reads own source via ast.parse() to extract module docstrings. get_module_map() returns {name: description}. format_self_awareness() produces prompt section showing source location and modules.
modules.archon/history.py: Conversation history persistence via JSONL session files in ~/.local/state/archon/history/. Functions: new_session_id(), save_message(), save_exchange(), load_session(), list_sessions(), delete_session(). Terminal chat uses timestamped session IDs (`YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS`); Telegram now reuses the same backend with chat-scoped IDs (`tg-<chat_id>-<timestamp>`).
modules.archon/prompt.py: Loads prompts/system.md template, injects: system_profile, tool_count, self_awareness, memory_summary, guidelines. ~2200 tokens total.
modules.archon/audio/tts.py: Gemini-based text-to-speech helper for Telegram voice replies: resolves Google API key from explicit arg/config/env, requests Gemini TTS audio output (`response_modalities=['AUDIO']` + `speech_config` with prebuilt voice), extracts inline audio payloads robustly from provider responses, normalizes to WAV bytes (wrapping raw PCM via stdlib `wave`), and provides optional ffmpeg-backed conversion (`convert_wav_to_ogg_opus`) for Telegram Phase C voice-note replies (`sendVoice`/OGG-Opus) with graceful fallback to WAV attachments.
modules.archon/calls/__init__.py: Call mission package marker for Archon's lightweight call-control plane.
modules.archon/calls/models.py: Dataclasses for call mission control-plane state plus call-related config (`CallsConfig`, `CallVoiceServiceConfig`, `TwilioCallsConfig`) and `CallMission` JSON serialization helpers.
modules.archon/calls/service_client.py: Stdlib local HTTP client helpers for the external voice runtime (`voice_service_health`, mission submit POST) with JSON parsing and friendly HTTP/network errors.
modules.archon/calls/store.py: XDG-backed file persistence for call missions/events (`~/.local/state/archon/calls/{missions,events}`): save/load/list mission JSON and append JSONL event logs.
modules.archon/calls/runner.py: Lightweight call orchestration wrappers: voice-service health/submit helpers plus Phase 1 mission start/status/list/cancel flows that validate inputs, persist file-based mission state, and submit scripted call missions to the local voice service.
modules.archon/tooling/call_service_tools.py: Registers `voice_service_status/start/stop` lifecycle tools with SAFE health reads and DANGEROUS confirmation gates for service start/stop (`systemd` mode first, subprocess mode stubbed).
modules.archon/tooling/call_mission_tools.py: Registers `call_mission_start/status/list/cancel` tools with local formatting and DANGEROUS confirmation gates for mission start/cancel actions.
modules.services/__init__.py: Top-level package marker for optional same-repo service runtimes kept outside Archon core.
modules.services/archon_voice/__init__.py: Archon voice service package marker (Phase 0/1 scripted call runtime).
modules.services/archon_voice/app.py: FastAPI voice runtime skeleton + Phase 1 scripted call path: `/health`, mission create/get endpoints, Twilio mission TwiML endpoint (`/twilio/missions/{id}/twiml`), Twilio speech gather endpoint (`/twilio/missions/{id}/gather`) for a lightweight multi-turn loop, and a Twilio status callback endpoint (`/twilio/status/{id}`) that updates mission status/provider SID from either JSON or Twilio form-encoded callbacks (`application/x-www-form-urlencoded`). The TwiML speech path includes lightweight goal-to-speech normalization for common meta-instructions (e.g. rewrites `ask the user how ...` into a spoken second-person question and extracts quoted text from `say exactly ...`), and now uses `<Gather input="speech">` for interactive-style goals before final `<Hangup/>`. Uses env-based Twilio credentials and stores mission state in-memory service-side (including turn counters/transcript).
modules.services/archon_voice/models.py: Service-side `VoiceMission` dataclass for mission payload normalization/serialization (`mission_id`, goal/target/status, provider SID, TwiML URL).
modules.services/archon_voice/twiml.py: Minimal TwiML XML builders for the voice runtime (`<Say>`, `<Gather>`, `<Hangup>`, `build_say_twiml`, `build_gather_twiml`) used by Phase 1 scripted and lightweight gather-loop call flows.
modules.services/archon_voice/twilio_client.py: Stdlib Twilio Voice REST client helper (`create_outbound_call`) using form-encoded POST + Basic auth; avoids the Twilio SDK dependency.
modules.services/archon_voice/tests/conftest.py: Service-test path bootstrap that adds the repo root to `sys.path` for service-local pytest runs in the isolated venv.
modules.services/archon_voice/README.md: Service-local README for the optional Archon voice runtime (setup, run, and test commands in the isolated venv).

prompts.prompts/system.md: Main system prompt template with {system_profile}, {tool_count}, {self_awareness}, {memory_summary}, {guidelines} placeholders.
prompts.prompts/guidelines.md: Behavioral rules: be concise, use shell for queries, prefer edit_file, save important context to memory, don't bypass safety, use `news_brief` for AI news requests, use `web_search` + `web_read` for current/latest web facts, use `delegate_code_task` (not raw shell) when asked to use Codex/Claude Code/OpenCode, and prefer continuing the existing same-repo Claude/OpenCode session (via `worker_send` / delegate auto-reuse) unless the user explicitly asks for a new/fresh session or one-shot. Use `worker_start`/`worker_send` plus `worker_poll`/`worker_status`/`worker_approve` for back-and-forth coding-worker collaboration (prefer `background=true` for approval-heavy or long turns). `delegate_code_task` supports `execution_mode=auto|oneshot|background`; leave `auto` unless the user explicitly wants a quick one-shot or explicitly wants a background session.

key_design_decisions: No frameworks - raw anthropic/openai/google-genai SDKs, full control

19 tools total - shell still handles most system tasks, while `news_brief` plus `web_search`/`web_read` give the agent native news/current-web retrieval and the worker tools (`delegate_code_task`, `worker_start/send/poll/cancel/status/list/approve/reconcile`) provide a normalized path to external coding workers with persistent session tracking, turn-based continuation (Claude + OpenCode), approval brokering, background execution with runtime PID/process-state visibility + quiet-time indicators + streamed runtime events, manual stale-session repair, a code-level delegate execution planner (`execution_mode=auto|oneshot|background`), and an auto-reroute path from clear continuation phrasing to `worker_send` for latest same-repo worker sessions
Safety gate in safety.py - cannot be modified by the agent itself (FORBIDDEN)
Prompts in prompts/ directory - editable without touching Python code
Internal message format is Anthropic-style - OpenAI and Google conversion happens in llm.py only
Google provider uses native SDK to avoid thought_signature issues with OpenAI-compat endpoint
base_url support on OpenAI provider enables OpenRouter, local models via LiteLLM proxy, etc.
Editable install - source changes take effect immediately, enables self-modification
known_gaps: Google streaming falls back to non-streaming (thought_signature preservation complexity); Anthropic and OpenAI stream natively
No fallback auto-switching on primary provider failure

Transient LLM retries are intentionally lightweight and currently implemented at the Agent layer (`run()` + `run_stream()`); lower-level `LLMClient` provider calls still do not have unified retry/backoff/error classification
No token-aware/context-budget management or summarization yet; current mitigation is a lightweight dual-budget history trim at turn start (message-count + approximate char-budget, token-free proxy)

Telegram dangerous-action approvals now support request-scoped inline button approvals (`callback_query`) with auto-replay and a lightweight 15-minute elevated mode; when a new blocked request arrives while pending, the pending payload is replaced with the latest request to avoid stale replay. Approval state is still in-memory only (pending approvals are lost on restart) and still tracks one pending approval per chat at a time

Telegram polling offset/session state is in-memory only (no resume after restart). Startup now skips queued pending updates to avoid replaying stale commands, but this also means offline messages are intentionally dropped on process start. Telegram chat history is now persisted to the shared JSONL history backend, but there is not yet a channel-aware `archon history` filter/view for `chat_id`/channel metadata

Telegram voice support now includes Phase C voice-note replies via optional ffmpeg conversion (WAV -> OGG/Opus + `sendVoice`) with automatic fallback to WAV document attachments if conversion/upload fails. The ffmpeg dependency is external (not a Python runtime dep) and raw audio is still not persisted.
News LLM timeout config (`news.llm.timeout_sec`) is defined but not yet enforced at the SDK call layer
Hugging Face papers parsing uses regex HTML scraping (best-effort) and may break if page markup changes
DuckDuckGo HTML search fallback in `archon/web/search.py` relies on page markup and may break; SearxNG or Brave providers are more reliable for production use
No built-in search result citation/evidence enforcement yet (tool returns URLs/snippets, but agent output is not eval-constrained)

External coding-worker delegation is still early-stage: Codex is implemented for one-shot runs (no Archon `worker_send` continuation path yet), while Claude Code supports turn-based follow-up via `--resume` and OpenCode supports turn-based follow-up via `opencode run --session <vendor_session_id>`. `delegate_code_task` sticky default-reuse currently applies to workers with continuation support (Claude/OpenCode); Codex still starts a new run per delegation until a resume path is implemented. OpenCode `serve/attach` warm-session mode is not implemented yet

Worker 'interactive' control is currently turn-based/resume-based, not full live subprocess session management: background execution exists via in-process daemon threads and streams runtime stdout/stderr line events + process lifecycle into `worker_poll`, but worker adapters still execute one subprocess turn at a time (no live stdin piping / incremental user messages mid-turn). `worker_status`/`worker_poll` expose runtime quiet-time indicators, and `worker_reconcile` provides manual recovery for orphaned/stale `running` sessions, but orphan detection is not yet automatic. Delegate execution planning/session-reuse policy is local-heuristic only (task text + mode + timeout + in-memory affinity), not channel-aware and not eval-tuned yet

Claude permission broker callbacks can create real approval requests now, but synchronous `worker_start`/`worker_send` calls still block while waiting; same-channel approvals are not possible until runs move to a background/live session manager (another channel can approve via `worker_approve` if the session is reserved/persisted)

Claude permission-broker path is version-sensitive: some installed Claude CLI builds may not expose `--permission-prompt-tool` (the adapter checks local `claude --help` and returns a clear unsupported message)

Memory retrieval/capture is still early-stage: indexed prefetch now injects lightweight lexical snippets (with basic freshness/stability metadata) into the turn system prompt, but retrieval remains heuristic (metadata scoring, fixed thresholds/limits) with no semantic embeddings; inbox apply is still simple append with no merge/conflict resolution or confidence verification workflow yet

Memory canonicalization/pointer maintenance is intentionally narrow in Phase 1 (explicit aliases like `system-profile.md` only); broader schema-driven normalization and merge policies are not implemented yet

Memory inbox remains Phase 1 review tooling: auto-capture now exists for explicit user preferences and some worker summaries, but there is still no dedupe/merge assistance UI, conflict resolution, or rich approval workflow; queued items are plain JSONL records and apply uses simple append semantics

Preference auto-capture is conservative and heuristic (pattern-based) and may miss nuanced preferences or occasionally queue wording that should remain ephemeral; there is no per-user approval UI for auto-captured inbox items yet

Realtime voice mission durability is still best-effort: `services/archon_voice` keeps live mission/runtime state in memory and Archon persistence is currently guaranteed when `call_mission_status` polls/merges service mission payloads; durable event-log persistence and richer list/status schema exposure remain future hardening work

2026-03-05: Root-cause analysis for recurring Google `400 INVALID_ARGUMENT` function-call ordering error in long conversations. Confirmed that `Agent.run()`/`run_stream()` currently call `_repair_history_tool_sequence()` before `_trim_history_if_needed()` (`archon/agent.py:60-62`, `126-128`), and trimming can reintroduce malformed function-call boundaries (e.g., history starting with `assistant tool_use` or orphaned `user tool_result`). Reproduced with local scripts showing post-trim sequences like `assistant tool_use -> user tool_result -> assistant text -> user`, which violate Google turn-order requirements and align with observed provider error.

2026-03-05: Fixed long-conversation Google function-calling protocol regression in `archon/agent.py` via TDD. Root cause: turn-start order ran `_repair_history_tool_sequence()` before `_trim_history_if_needed()`, and trimming could reintroduce malformed tool-call boundaries (leading `assistant/tool_use` or orphaned `user/tool_result`). Fix: run trim before repair in `run()` and `run_stream()`, and harden repair logic to keep tool-use/tool-result pairs only when assistant tool_use is both preceded by a user turn and immediately followed by a user tool_result turn; invalid fragments are dropped. Added regression tests `test_run_repairs_tool_sequence_after_message_count_trim` and `test_run_repairs_orphaned_tool_result_after_char_trim` in `tests/test_agent.py`, plus helper assertion `assert_tool_sequence_well_formed`.
